hypothesis:
Given that the paper demonstrates hybrid CNN-GRU models can predict polymer-surface binding free energies from coarse-grained simulations but acknowledges these models cannot reproduce experimental free energies quantitatively, a novel hypothesis extending this work is:

**Hypothesis:** Transfer learning from coarse-grained to atomistic resolution by pre-training the hybrid CNN-GRU-Transformer architecture on extensive coarse-grained polymer-surface datasets and fine-tuning on limited atomistic simulation data will enable quantitatively accurate prediction of experimental binding free energies while preserving the sequence-composition design principles learned at the coarse-grained level.
